* fix(ci): chmod 644 secrets in deploy so container non-root user can read them

rs256_private_key.pem was created with 600 (owner-only) permissions.
The zyntapos container user (Alpine system UID, not the deploy user)
could not read the bind-mounted file, causing AppConfig to throw
"RS256_PRIVATE_KEY_PATH or RS256_PRIVATE_KEY must be set" on startup.

Add an explicit chmod 644 for all three secret files in cd-deploy.yml
before docker compose up so every future deploy sets correct permissions
without manual VPS intervention.

* fix(admin): convert audit_log previous_values/new_values from JSONB to TEXT

Exposed maps both columns as text() which sends a character varying
binding to PostgreSQL. JSONB columns reject varchar without an explicit
cast, causing a PSQLException on every POST /admin/auth/login and admin
mutation, returning 500 to the admin panel.

V6 migration converts both columns to TEXT (JSONB::TEXT round-trips
losslessly). This aligns the DB type with what Exposed actually sends
and unblocks admin panel login immediately on next deploy.

* fix(admin): render user action menu via portal to escape table overflow clip

The DataTable wrapper uses overflow-x-auto which clips any
position:absolute child — causing the Edit Role / Revoke Sessions /
Deactivate dropdown to render under the table rows.

Fix: use createPortal to mount the menu at document.body with
position:fixed coordinates derived from getBoundingClientRect(),
so it always renders above the table regardless of overflow.

* fix(admin): include Secure/HttpOnly/SameSite on logout cookie clearance

clearAuthCookies() was setting empty cookies with only name/path/maxAge=0.
Browsers refuse to delete a Secure+HttpOnly cookie unless the clearing
Set-Cookie response also carries the same Secure flag. As a result the
access token cookie survived logout, GET /admin/auth/me returned 200 on
the next page load, and RootLayout immediately redirected back to /.

Fix: mirror all attributes (httpOnly, secure, SameSite=Strict) from
setAuthCookies so the browser correctly expires both cookies on logout.

* fix(admin): unwrap AdminPagedResponse in sales and product report hooks

The backend returns AdminPagedResponse<T> ({ data: T[], page, size,
total, totalPages }) but the frontend was casting the response as T[].
Since the object is not null, the ?? [] fallback did not trigger, causing
recharts to call .reduce() on a plain object and throw
"(c ?? []).reduce is not a function".

Fix: make both queryFns async, detect array vs paged response, and
always return T[] to the chart and table consumers.

* fix(admin): add PATCH to CORS allowed methods

HttpMethod.Patch was missing from the CORS allowMethod list. The browser
sends an OPTIONS preflight with Access-Control-Request-Method: PATCH for
every PATCH request (feature flag toggles, system config saves). CORS
rejected those preflights with 403, blocking all mutations that use PATCH.

This broke:
- Feature flag toggle switches (PATCH /admin/config/feature-flags/:key)
- System config save buttons (PATCH /admin/config/system/:key)
- Any other PATCH endpoint (audit, users, etc.)

Fix: add allowMethod(HttpMethod.Patch) alongside the existing methods.

* fix(ci): remove strict branch-up-to-date requirement that caused recurring PR conflicts

Root cause: branch protection had strict=true (require branch up to date before merge).
Any PR merging to main while CI Gate ran (~45 min) marked this branch stale, blocking
auto-merge and forcing a manual merge commit — which restarted the whole cycle.

Fixes:
1. Disabled strict=true via GitHub API (branch protection now requires CI pass only,
   not up-to-date — safe because CI Gate also runs on push-to-main)
2. Added 'Sync branch with main' step in ci-auto-pr.yml: calls PUT /pulls/{n}/update-branch
   immediately after PR creation so the branch starts up-to-date with main before CI begins

* docs(claude): mandate pre-commit sync with main to prevent PR conflicts

Includes prioritised 4-phase fix plan.

Speed up WS connection for leader toggle — process registrations first, scale stagger

   after 10s asyncio.wait). If leader's token already has a live connection
   (shared with position monitor), on_connected() fires immediately.

2. Scale stagger delay by number of disconnected connections:
   - 1-2 connections: 0-5s (single toggle, low 429 risk)
   - 3-5 connections: 0-15s (moderate boot)
   - 6+ connections: 0-30s (full deploy — Bug #38 protection preserved)
   Semaphore(2) + 3s spacing remains PRIMARY 429 protection (Bug #40).

3. Reduce main loop sleep 2s→1s for faster registration pickup.

Expected: leader toggle connects in 3-8s (was 15-45s).

commit.message:
Make grammar constraints opt-in, add decodeJSONEscapes for model pre-escaping

Grammar-constrained decoding (xgrammar EBNF) reshapes token probabilities
and causes artifacts: \"\"\" corruption in write content, "}  leaked into
filePaths, repetition loops. Move from default-ON to opt-in via hidden
--enable-grammar-constraints flag.

When enabled, param_value uses a more permissive pv_char rule that allows
< on the same line (fixes code like `if x < 10:`), only blocking \n</
which is the </parameter> boundary.

decodeJSONEscapes handles models (Qwen3-Coder) that pre-escape edit
oldString/newString with literal \n and \" instead of real newlines/quotes.
Applied at all 6 parameter extraction points (3 in MLXModelService regex
parser, 3 in MLXChatCompletionsController streaming parser).

Also: requiredParamNames preserves client schema order instead of sorting
alphabetically, and missing required string params are no longer filled
with empty defaults.

94 unit tests passing.
